en: In those days Hezekiah became sick and was at the point of death. And Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz came to him, and said to him, "Thus says the LORD: Set your house in order, for you shall die, you shall not recover."
zh: 那時，希西家患了重病，快要死去。亞摩斯的兒子以賽亞先知來見他，對他說：“耶和華這樣說：‘你要把你的家整頓妥當，因為你快要死去，不能存活。’”

en: Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the LORD,
zh: 希西家就轉臉向牆，對耶和華禱告、

en: and said, "Please, O LORD, remember how I have walked before you in faithfulness and with a whole heart, and have done what is good in your sight." And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
zh: 說：“耶和華啊！求你記念我在你面前怎樣誠誠實實、以純全的心來行事，又作你眼中看為善的事。”然後，希西家就痛哭起來。

en: Then the word of the LORD came to Isaiah:
zh: 耶和華的話臨到以賽亞說：

en: "Go and say to Hezekiah, Thus says the LORD, the God of David your father: I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Behold, I will add fifteen years to your life.
zh: “你去告訴希西家：‘耶和華你祖先大衛的 神這樣說：我聽見了你的禱告，看見了你的眼淚；看哪！我必在你的壽數上加添十五年。

en: I will deliver you and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria, and will defend this city.
zh: 我必救你和這城脫離亞述王的手；我必保護這城。’

en: "This shall be the sign to you from the LORD, that the LORD will do this thing that he has promised:
zh: 以下就是耶和華給你的兆頭，為要證明耶和華必成全他所說過的。

en: Behold, I will make the shadow cast by the declining sun on the dial of Ahaz turn back ten steps." So the sun turned back on the dial the ten steps by which it had declined.
zh: 看哪！我要使亞哈斯日晷上那向前進的日影往後退十度。”於是那向前進的日影，果然在日晷上往後退了十度。

en: A writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, after he had been sick and had recovered from his sickness:
zh: 猶大王希西家從病患中痊愈以後，就寫了這詩：

en: I said, In the middle of my days I must depart; I am consigned to the gates of Sheol for the rest of my years.
zh: 我曾說：在我盛年之際， 我竟要進入陰間之門； 我餘剩的年日，竟被奪去。

en: I said, I shall not see the LORD, the LORD in the land of the living; I shall look on man no more among the inhabitants of the world.
zh: 我曾說：在活人之地， 我必不得見耶和華； 在世間的居民中，我必不再見到任何人。

en: My dwelling is plucked up and removed from me like a shepherd's tent; like a weaver I have rolled up my life; he cuts me off from the loom; from day to night you bring me to an end;
zh: 我的住處被拔起，遷離了我，像牧人的帳棚一樣； 我捲起我的性命，像織布的捲布一樣； 他把我從機頭剪斷； 一日之間，他必使我生命終結。

en: I calmed myself until morning; like a lion he breaks all my bones; from day to night you bring me to an end.
zh: 我不斷哀歎，直到早晨， 他像獅子一般，折斷我所有的骨頭； 一日之間，他必使我生命終結。

en: Like a swallow or a crane I chirp; I moan like a dove. My eyes are weary with looking upward. O Lord, I am oppressed; be my pledge of safety!
zh: 我像燕子呻吟，像白鶴鳴叫， 又像鴿子哀鳴。 我的眼睛因仰望高處而疲倦； 耶和華啊！我受欺壓，求你作我的保障。

en: What shall I say? For he has spoken to me, and he himself has done it. I walk slowly all my years because of the bitterness of my soul.
zh: 我可說甚麼呢？ 他應許我的，已給我成全了； 因我的心曾受過苦楚，所以我要謹慎地度我所有的年日。

en: O Lord, by these things men live, and in all these is the life of my spirit. Oh restore me to health and make me live!
zh: 主啊！人活著是在於這些， 我的靈存活，也在於這些。 所以求你使我恢復健康，使我存活。

en: Behold, it was for my welfare that I had great bitterness; but in love you have delivered my life from the pit of destruction, for you have cast all my sins behind your back.
zh: 看哪！我受盡苦楚，為要使我得平安； 你保全我的性命，脫離毀滅的坑， 因為你把我的一切罪都丟在你的背後。

en: For Sheol does not thank you; death does not praise you; those who go down to the pit do not hope for your faithfulness.
zh: 陰間不能稱謝你， 死亡不能讚美你， 下坑的人都不能仰望你的信實。

en: The living, the living, he thanks you, as I do this day; the father makes known to the children your faithfulness.
zh: 只有活人才能稱謝你，像我今日一樣； 作父親的，要讓兒女知道你的信實。

en: The LORD will save me, and we will play my music on stringed instruments all the days of our lives, at the house of the LORD.
zh: 耶和華必拯救我， 因此我們要一生一世在耶和華的殿中， 用絲弦的樂器彈奏我的詩歌。

en: Now Isaiah had said, "Let them take a cake of figs and apply it to the boil, that he may recover."
zh: 以賽亞說：“叫人拿一塊無花果餅來，貼在瘡上，王就必痊愈。”

en: Hezekiah also had said, "What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the LORD?"
zh: 希西家問：“我能上耶和華的殿去，有甚麼兆頭呢？”
